name: ntfy
description: Simple HTTP-based pub-sub notification service. Send push notifications to your phone or desktop via scripts.
summary: ntfy has seen rapid development in early 2026, with v2.21.0 (released 2026-03-30) adding email verification via `smtp-sender-verify` after ntfy.sh's AWS SES account was suspended due to unsolicited email abuse. v2.20.0 introduced S3-compatible object storage for attachments, and v2.18.0 (released 2026-03-07) added experimental PostgreSQL support as an alternative to the SQLite backend — a major architectural change. Read replica support was added in v2.19.0. Full release notes are available at https://github.com/binwiederhier/ntfy/releases.

answers.What is the current license?: Apache 2.0 (server) and GPL-2.0 (client) — check repository for exact details
answers.Is the project actively maintained?: Yes — multiple releases in March 2026 alone
answers.Does it support authentication and acces: Yes — user management, access control, and authorization queries supported

breaking_excerpts: v2.20.0: ntfy will take full control over the attachment directory or S3 bucket, deleting files matching the message ID format (^[A-Za-z0-9]{12}$) with no DB entries — do not reuse the attachment-cache-dir for other purposes.
v2.21.0: ntfy.sh now requires email address verification via smtp-sender-verify before sending email notifications, due to AWS SES suspension.
v2.18.0: Experimental PostgreSQL backend introduced as an alternative to SQLite via database-url config option — significant architectural change requiring migration.

alternatives.name: Pushover
alternatives.review: Paid SaaS, simple API, iOS/Android apps.

alternatives.name: Gotify
alternatives.review: Self-hosted only, real-time push via WebSocket.

alternatives.name: Apprise
alternatives.review: Apprise is a versatile open-source notification library supporting dozens of services but is a library/CLI tool rather than a standalone pub-sub server with its own push infrastructure.

alternatives.name: Pushbullet
alternatives.review: Pushbullet provides cross-device notifications with file and link sharing features but is a proprietary cloud-only service with a restrictive free tier compared to ntfy's fully open approach.

alternatives.name: Bark
alternatives.review: Bark offers a simple self-hosted HTTP push notification service similar to ntfy but is iOS-only, making it unsuitable for Android or desktop users.
